description_long: This is a British Rail Class 08 diesel-electric shunting locomotive, characterized by its compact, boxy body and robust construction. The locomotive is painted in a dark blue livery with yellow and black hazard stripes on the front and rear ends, enhancing visibility and safety. The sides feature a series of rectangular windows and doors, as well as the British Rail logo and identification number. The undercarriage is equipped with large metal wheels, visible suspension components, and various mechanical details. The front and rear both have prominent buffers, couplers, and steps for crew access. The overall design is utilitarian, built for maneuvering rail cars in rail yards and industrial settings.
description: A compact, blue and yellow British Rail shunting locomotive with hazard stripes, side windows, and detailed undercarriage. Features include prominent buffers, couplers, and access steps.
description_view_0: Front view showing yellow and black hazard stripes, central coupler, and buffers.
description_view_1: Side view displaying blue body, British Rail logo, windows, doors, and undercarriage details.
description_view_2: Rear view with yellow doors, hazard stripes, and rear coupler and buffers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with similar features to view 1, including windows, doors, and undercarriage.
attribute_door_placement: doors at each end and on both sides
attribute_window_arrangement: spaced rectangular windows on both sides
